commands.review_summary:
The branch adds pre-normalization quarantine for non-serializable runtime tool schemas in assistant attempts and compaction, reserves quarantined active tool names for bundled MCP/LSP materialization, and adds focused schema/runtime coverage.

PR surface: Source +140, Tests +262. Total +402 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main normalizes raw active and bundled tool schemas before quarantine, so a circular or otherwise non-serializable schema can fail before diagnostics.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer compatibility acceptance and normal landing validation, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid runtime tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

Quarantine active runtime tools whose schemas cannot safely reach provider/runtime schema projection, including assistant-turn startup and compaction.

This also covers the earlier tool-parameter normalization boundary: schemas that are not JSON-serializable are left untouched by schema normalization so the runtime quarantine path can omit and report them instead of crashing before diagnostics.

Healthy sibling tools continue. Quarantined active tool names remain reserved for bundled MCP/LSP materialization, so a bundled tool cannot silently reuse the name of an omitted active tool.

The test fixtures use neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names. The branch was pruned after review to keep the PR focused: current diff is 12 files, +464/-62.

## Verification

- Rebased onto current `origin/main` `2f7e6ec196fd`; pushed head `128879b491d`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts`: passed 2 shards / 166 tests in 19.93s after the final rebase.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...`: passed for all 12 PR files.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`: passed.
- Changed-file scrub for the originally reported fixture name: clean.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch correct.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`: currently blocked before lint execution by current-main boundary DTS drift in `src/infra/device-auth-store.ts`, introduced outside this PR's touched files. The same branch had targeted oxlint green before that upstream drift.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: passed earlier on run `run_7403d1c41a29`, lease `cbx_26569933db9f` / `blue-crab`, provider `aws`, command 8m50s, total 11m27s, lanes `core` and `coreTests`, lease stopped=true.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A single active tool with a schema that cannot survive provider/runtime schema projection is quarantined before assistant session creation instead of crashing the whole assistant turn.

Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest/format/review proof through repo wrappers; remote Linux changed gate through AWS Crabbox before later unrelated main drift.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Verification above.

Evidence after fix: Focused runner coverage shows session creation receives only healthy sibling tools when an active tool has an unsupported or non-serializable schema. Schema-normalizer coverage verifies non-JSON-serializable schemas are left for runtime quarantine instead of crashing early. Attempt and compaction coverage verifies quarantined active names remain reserved for bundled MCP/LSP runtimes.

Observed result after fix: Healthy tools continue; active bad tools are reported through the existing trusted diagnostic/quarantine path; bundled tools cannot take over quarantined active names.

What was not tested: Live provider calls.
target.author: vincentkoc
